description: Add comprehensive unit and E2E tests for the tools enable/disable flow. A code review found that while the implementation is correct (dual gating in tools/list + tools/call, config persistence, MCP client notification), test coverage has critical gaps: no test verifies that disabled tools are rejected at dispatch time, no test covers the toggle-all flow, and no test confirms browser tools bypass enable/disable by design.

userStories.title: Extract getEnabledToolsList helper and add unit tests for disabled tool filtering in tools/list
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the tools/list filtering logic extracted into a testable helper function so that I can verify disabled tools are excluded from the MCP tool list without needing a full MCP Server instance.
acceptanceCriteria: A new exported function `getEnabledToolsList(state)` exists in mcp-setup.ts that returns the array of tool objects (name, description, inputSchema)
The `registerMcpHandlers` tools/list handler delegates to `getEnabledToolsList` instead of inlining the logic
Unit test: all tools enabled (default) — all plugin tools + all browser tools appear in the list
Unit test: one plugin tool disabled via toolConfig — that tool is excluded, others remain
Unit test: all plugin tools disabled — only browser tools appear
Unit test: browser tools always appear regardless of toolConfig entries matching their names
Unit test: multiple plugins with mixed enabled/disabled tools — correct filtering per plugin
Unit test: empty plugins map returns only browser tools
Existing behavior is unchanged — tools/list responses are identical before and after extraction
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes

userStories.title: Add unit tests verifying disabled tool dispatch returns error in tools/call
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests proving that the tools/call handler rejects calls to disabled tools with 'Tool X is disabled' so that the security gate is verified at the unit level.
acceptanceCriteria: A new exported function `checkToolCallable(state, toolName)` exists in mcp-setup.ts that returns `{ ok: true, pluginName, toolName }` or `{ ok: false, error: string, isNotFound?: boolean }`
The tools/call handler delegates to `checkToolCallable` for the lookup + enabled check before dispatching
Unit test: tool exists and is enabled — returns ok: true with correct pluginName and toolName
Unit test: tool exists but is disabled — returns ok: false with error containing 'disabled'
Unit test: tool does not exist (not in toolLookup) — returns ok: false with error containing 'not found'
Unit test: browser tool name is not checked by checkToolCallable (browser tools are handled separately before this check)
Existing tools/call behavior is unchanged
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes

userStories.title: E2E test: disabled tool call via MCP client returns error
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test proving that when a tool is disabled via the side panel, calling it through the MCP client returns an error, so that the full-stack security gate is verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: New test in e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts in a new describe block 'Side panel — disabled tool dispatch rejection'
Test starts MCP server with e2e-test plugin, connects extension, opens side panel
Test opens a matching tab so the e2e-test plugin reaches 'ready' state
Test verifies tool call succeeds initially (e2e-test_echo with args returns output)
Test disables the echo tool via the side panel toggle UI
Test verifies MCP tools/list no longer includes e2e-test_echo (polls with expect.poll)
Test calls e2e-test_echo via MCP client and verifies isError: true with content containing 'disabled'
Test re-enables the echo tool via the side panel toggle
Test verifies tool call succeeds again after re-enabling
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes

userStories.title: E2E test: toggle-all switch disables and re-enables all plugin tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test proving that the plugin-level toggle-all switch removes all tools from MCP tools/list and re-adds them when toggled back, so that the setAllToolsEnabled flow is verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: New test in e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts in a new describe block 'Side panel — toggle all tools'
Test starts MCP server with e2e-test plugin, connects extension, opens side panel
Test verifies all e2e-test plugin tools initially appear in MCP tools/list
Test clicks the plugin card's master toggle switch (the ToggleSwitch in the header row)
Test verifies all e2e-test plugin tools disappear from MCP tools/list (polls with expect.poll)
Test verifies config.json on disk has all e2e-test tools set to false (polls with expect.poll)
Test clicks the master toggle again to re-enable all tools
Test verifies all e2e-test plugin tools reappear in MCP tools/list
Test verifies browser tools are NOT affected by the toggle (they remain in tools/list throughout)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/side-panel-plugin-toggle.e2e.ts. The master toggle is in PluginCard.tsx — it's the ToggleSwitch rendered in the header row (`<ToggleSwitch ... onClick={handleToggleAll} ariaLabel='Toggle all tools for ${plugin.name}' />`). 

Locate it via: `sidePanelPage.locator('button[role="switch"][aria-label="Toggle all tools for e2e-test"]')`.

The e2e-test plugin tools can be read dynamically via `readPluginToolNames()` from fixtures.ts. These return prefixed names like 'e2e-test_echo', 'e2e-test_get_status', etc.

Browser tool names are listed in `BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES` from helpers.ts. Verify at least a few of these remain in tools/list after disabling all plugin tools.

Follow the same test setup pattern as the existing toggle test: `startMcpServer`, `createMcpClient`, `launchExtensionContext`, `setupAdapterSymlink`, `waitForExtensionConnected`, `waitForLog`, `mcpClient.initialize()`, `openSidePanel`.

userStories.title: Unit test: tool config survives config file round-trip (save + load)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a unit test proving that disabled tool state persists through a config save → load cycle so that tool config survives MCP server restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: New test in platform/mcp-server/src/config.test.ts
Test creates a temp config directory, writes a config with some tools disabled, loads it back, and verifies the disabled tools are present with value false
Test verifies that enabled tools (value true) are preserved
Test verifies that tools not in the config default to enabled (isToolEnabled returns true for absent keys)
Test uses saveConfig to write, then loadConfig to read, verifying round-trip fidelity
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/config.test.ts. Look at the existing tests in that file for the pattern (they already use temp directories with OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR).

The test should:
1. Set up a temp dir with OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR env var
2. Save a config with `saveConfig(state, { plugins: [], tools: { 'slack_send': false, 'slack_read': true }, secret: 'test' })`
3. Load it back with `loadConfig()`
4. Assert `loaded.tools['slack_send'] === false`
5. Assert `loaded.tools['slack_read'] === true`
6. Verify `isToolEnabled({ toolConfig: loaded.tools } as any, 'slack_send') === false`
7. Verify `isToolEnabled({ toolConfig: loaded.tools } as any, 'unknown_tool') === true` (absent = enabled)

Remember to clean up: restore OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR and rmSync the temp dir in afterEach.
